risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study... During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks. ... At the end of year 1, patients on placebo were re-randomized to peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg dosed every 2 weeks or every 4 weeks." Comment: The paper states that participants were randomized and re-randomized, but it does not describe how the allocation sequence was generated (e.g. computer-generated random numbers, random number table, block randomization). With 'randomized' alone and no further method details, random sequence generation is unclear.

Quote: "ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study... During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive..." Comment: The report confirms randomization and blinding, but gives no explicit description of how allocation was concealed from the person enrolling participants (e.g. central randomization, web/telephone system, pharmacy-controlled allocation, or sequentially numbered opaque sealed envelopes). Therefore, recruiter foreknowledge of the upcoming assignment cannot be judged.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study with a 1 year placebo-controlled period." and "To maintain the blind, patients received either peginterferon beta-1a or placebo every 2 weeks. Those randomized to the peginterferon beta-1a dosed every 4 weeks group received alternate injections of placebo and peginterferon beta-1a every 2 weeks." Comment: The report explicitly states the study was double-blind and describes a matching dosing schedule used to maintain blinding, indicating participants and study personnel were intended to remain unaware of assignment. No evidence is presented that blinding was broken; therefore performance bias is low overall, including for clinically influenceable outcomes such as relapse.

Quote: "ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study with a 1 year placebo-controlled period... To maintain the blind, patients received either peginterferon beta-1a or placebo every 2 weeks." Quote: "Serum concentrations of peginterferon beta-1a were evaluated using an enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA)... Serum neopterin was measured using a validated competition ELISA." Comment: The outcomes assessed in this report were primarily lab-based PK/PD and immunogenicity measures, so the assessor is best classified as a lab/imaging system/laboratory assay. These are objective measurements obtained with validated assays and are unlikely to be influenced by knowledge of allocation. The study also states it was double-blind, supporting blinding of outcome assessment.

Quote: "A full description of participant flow in the ADVANCE study and details of baseline demographic and clinical characteristics in each treatment group have been published previously 4. Forty-four patients consented to the intensive PK and PD subgroup at year 1 of the ADVANCE study. Among these, four male and 21 female patients received peginterferon beta-1a (every 2 weeks n = 12; every 4 weeks n = 13) and had sufficient time point samples for evaluation of PK and PD parameters." Comment: This report gives only subgroup counts for the intensive PK/PD analysis (44 consented; 25 analysed, split 12 vs 13) but does not report per-arm randomized-to-analysed numbers for the main ADVANCE clinical outcomes of interest (relapse, disease progression) at the analysed time point, nor reasons for missing outcome data by arm. Because the paper refers participant flow to a previous publication and does not provide the necessary attrition numbers here, attrition bias for the outcomes of interest cannot be judged confidently from this article alone.
